leadText: Boomburst (Japanese: ばくおんぱ Sound Wave Bomb) is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation VI.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Boomburst does damage to all adjacent Pokémon, including allies. Boomburst is a sound-based move . Boomburst can hit Pokémon even if they are behind a substitute. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 140 168 Cooldown 12s Move wind-up time 20 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA The user attacks everything around it with the destructive power of a terrible, explosive sound. S V The user attacks everything around it with the destructive power of a terrible explosive sound. ZA The user attacks by unleashing the destructive power of a terrible explosive sound across a wide area.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD It damages all targets in the room, including your teammates, but not you. * It damages all Pokémon in the same room except for you. *

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Boomburst has the highest base power of all moves with no negative effects on the user, and the highest base power of all sound-based moves, excluding Echoed Voice , which can reach a base power of 200. Boomburst was the first Generation VI move to be seen in the animated series . It was used by Alexa 's Noivern in Celebrating the Hero's Comet! , prior to the release of Pokémon X and Y .
